question.text: A bank offers a fixed deposit at 12% p.a. The effective annual rate when interest is compounded monthly is closest to:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Effective rate = (1 + r/m)^m - 1 = (1 + 0.12/12)^12 - 1 = (1.01)^12 - 1 = 1.12683 - 1 = 12.68%. **The Trap:** 12.36% results from semi-annual compounding. Monthly compounding gives a higher effective rate than semi-annual. **Cross-Exam Context:** Effective annual rate is tested in X-A (Ch 2), IV (Interest Rate Derivatives), and IX (bond pricing). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Compare investments using effective annual rate, not stated rate — monthly compounding at 12% beats quarterly compounding at 12%.
